---
title: "Better Word Template Playground"
subtitle: "Custom OpenXML Pandoc Template"
title-logo: "![](logo.svg){height=2cm}"
author:
  - name:
      given: Robin
      family: Everhart
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    email: robin.everhart@example.org
    corresponding: true
    affiliations:
      - ref: uni
      - ref: lab
  - name: Sam Okoro
    affiliations:
      - ref: uni
  - name: Priya Chandra
    affiliations:
      - ref: lab
affiliations:
  - id: uni
    name: Fictional University
    department: Department of Examples
    city: Springfield
    country: Freedonia
  - id: lab
    name: Placeholder Research Institute
    city: Metropolis
    country: Ruritania
date: today
date-modified: today
doi: 10.5555/12345678
description: |
  A short description of the document, rendered in the title block just like
  Quarto's HTML format.
categories:
  - Quarto
  - OpenXML
  - Word
keywords:
  - docx
  - template
  - title block
abstract: |
  This document is a playground for Quarto's `docx` format driven by a custom
  OpenXML Pandoc template. It shows how the title block, author and affiliation
  metadata, page geometry, and landscape sections can be customised structurally,
  in the spirit of the HTML and Typst templates.
docx-title-page-break: true
format:
  better-word-docx: default
---

## Title Block

The custom template builds a journal-style title block from Quarto's normalised
`by-author` and `by-affiliation` metadata:

- authors on a single line with superscript affiliation numbers;
- a superscript `*` on the corresponding author;
- clickable ORCID and email via field-code hyperlinks (no relationship needed);
- a numbered affiliations list and a correspondence line.

Compare the front matter above with the rendered `.docx` title block to see the
effect.

## Page Geometry

The base page size and margins are defined once, as the shared `docx-page-width`,
`docx-page-height`, and `docx-page-margin` metadata (in twips). The template
consumes them in the base `w:sectPr`, and the `landscape-page-size.lua` filter
reads the same values so landscape sections stay in sync.

## Landscape Sections

The section below is landscape. Its page size derives from the shared geometry
(A4 landscape), and the preceding portrait section keeps its size and margins,
which the built-in landscape filter otherwise drops.

::: {.landscape}

| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 | Column 4 | Column 5 | Column 6 |
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| A        | B        | C        | D        | E        | F        |
| G        | H        | I        | J        | K        | L        |

:::

## Custom Styles

Pandoc applies named Word styles from the reference document and creates any
that are missing:

::: {custom-style="Intense Quote"}

This paragraph uses the `Intense Quote` Word style through a `custom-style` div.

:::

## Text Formatting

The format supports standard Markdown formatting:

- **Bold text** for emphasis.
- *Italic text* for subtle emphasis.
- `Code` for inline code.

## Conclusion

Everything below the title block mirrors Pandoc's `default.openxml`, so the body
behaves exactly as Quarto expects.
The template takes over the front matter and the base section properties, which
is where advanced customisation lives.
